contact.title: Contact
contact.description: Contact form for team opportunities, technical collaborations or work proposals.

contentSection.heading: How I Collaborate
contentSection.paragraph1: My integration process starts with a technical conversation to understand the architecture, stack, and team goals. I adapt quickly to existing codebases, coding conventions, and established workflows. I value clear communication, constructive feedback, and continuous improvement of both the product and the development process. During my first weeks on a new team I focus on understanding the business domain, the project structure, and the architectural decisions already made. I do not arrive imposing my way of working but rather join the existing flow and contribute incremental improvements from within. I review available documentation, test suites, PR history, and project configurations to grasp the full context before suggesting changes. My goal is to be productive from day one without generating noise or friction, contributing clean code, relevant questions, and a collaborative attitude that strengthens the team.
contentSection.paragraph2: I thrive in environments that prioritize software quality, automated testing, and well-reasoned technical decisions. My experience covers full-stack applications with React and Node.js, CLI tools, process automation, and relational database systems. If your team works with TypeScript, continuous integration, and clean architecture, I can contribute from the first sprint. I have worked both in small startups where speed is essential and in larger teams with established code review and deployment processes. In every case my approach is the same: write code that another developer can understand without my explanation, document important decisions, and leave each module in better shape than I found it. I have experience with agile methodologies, task estimation, pair programming, and constructive retrospectives. I do not need constant supervision and take ownership of what I build, but I also know when to ask for help when domain complexity demands it.
contentSection.paragraph3: Testing discipline is a fundamental part of how I work. I write tests first when fixing bugs (test-driven debugging) and tests after to verify new behaviors, ensuring that each suite tests functionality from the user's perspective rather than implementation details. During code review I focus on code clarity, edge case coverage, and architectural consistency, providing actionable feedback and receiving critique with the same openness I expect from others. I believe peer review is not a gatekeeping exercise but a tool for collective learning. Regarding CI/CD, I design pipelines that run linters, type-checking, and tests in parallel, with automated deployments that include schema validation and post-deploy health checks. Team communication I handle with transparency: daily task updates, technical discussions documented in PRs, and architectural decisions recorded for traceability.

hero.label: Open to joining a team
hero.titleAccessible: I want to join a team and build something precise.
hero.description: I'm looking to join a team where I can translate complex requirements into clean, accessible, high-performance interfaces. My profile combines strong TypeScript, React, and Node.js engineering skills with clear communication and product-oriented thinking. Reach out if you want to discuss a role, a technical collaboration, or a concrete opportunity.
